api/WorldLib.Utils.TransmutableHashSet-2.title: Class TransmutableHashSet<TStorage, TExposed> | WorldLib
api/WorldLib.Utils.TransmutableHashSet-2.summary:
Class TransmutableHashSet Namespace WorldLib.Utils Assembly WorldLib.dll Represents a live, transmutable view over an underlying unordered collection of serialized items with set semantics — no ordering, no duplicates. Each element is transformed via a codec between the storage representation (TStorage) and the exposed runtime representation (TExposed). public sealed class TransmutableHashSet Type Parameters TStorage The serialized storage representation used by the underlying data source. This is typically a primitive or identifier type (for example string). TExposed The runtime representation exposed by the set interface. This is commonly a higher-level object resolved from the storage representation. Inheritance object TransmutableHashSet Inherited Members object.ToString() object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() Remarks This type acts as a projection layer between an underlying set-backed storage container and a higher-level runtime representation. Unlike typical adapter collections, this set does not maintain its own backing storage. Instead, all operations delegate directly to externally supplied accessors. This ensures that the collection always reflects the current state of the underlying storage, even if modified externally (for example by other mods). Each element is encoded and decoded on demand using the supplied codec delegates. No caching is performed by this class. Implementations using hash-set-backed storage typically achieve the following complexity characteristics:
  • Add: O(1)
  • Remove: O(1)
  • Contains: O(1)
  • Clear: O(n)
Constructors TransmutableHashSet(Func, Func>, Action, Func, Action, Func, Func) Represents a live, transmutable view over an underlying unordered collection of serialized items with set semantics — no ordering, no duplicates. Each element is transformed via a codec between the storage representation (TStorage) and the exposed runtime representation (TExposed). public TransmutableHashSet(Func count, Func> getAll, Action add, Func remove, Action clear, Func decode, Func encode) Parameters count Func A delegate returning the number of elements currently present in the underlying storage collection. Cannot be null. getAll Func> A delegate returning an enumerable over all serialized elements currently present in the underlying storage. Used internally for iteration and containment checks. Cannot be null. add Action A delegate that inserts a serialized element into the underlying storage. Cannot be null. remove Func A delegate that removes a serialized element from the underlying storage, returning true if the element was present and removed. Cannot be null. clear Action A delegate that removes all elements from the underlying storage. Cannot be null. decode Func A delegate that converts a storage element into its exposed runtime form. Cannot be null. encode Func A delegate that converts an exposed element back into its storage form. Cannot be null. Remarks This type acts as a projection layer between an underlying set-backed storage container and a higher-level runtime representation. Unlike typical adapter collections, this set does not maintain its own backing storage. Instead, all operations delegate directly to externally supplied accessors. This ensures that the collection always reflects the current state of the underlying storage, even if modified externally (for example by other mods). Each element is encoded and decoded on demand using the supplied codec delegates. No caching is performed by this class. Implementations using hash-set-backed storage typically achieve the following complexity characteristics:
  • Add: O(1)
  • Remove: O(1)
  • Contains: O(1)
  • Clear: O(n)
